caption: I watch as the customer gets up from the table and moves away, apparently preparing to leave the seating area. I then turn and head back toward the kitchen/work area, likely to resume cafe duties after seeing the customer off.
I approach the seated customer at the table as if checking on or serving her, and she responds by leaning forward and standing up from her chair, apparently preparing to leave or follow me.

I watch a seated customer at the table as she leans forward and stands up, apparently preparing to leave or move away from the table. I then turn and walk toward the back/kitchen area, likely to continue cafe work while the customer heads off.

I move away from the table toward the back kitchen area, likely to continue cafe work or prepare something, while the customer stands up from her chair and steps aside as if getting ready to leave or follow.
I approach the seated customer as if to assist or check on her, while she stands up from the table and moves past me, appearing ready to leave the seating area or follow me.
I approach a seated customer at the table as if checking on or taking their order, and the customer rises from the chair and turns toward me, appearing ready to speak or respond.

I move away from the table and head toward the back kitchen area, likely to continue service or prepare something, while the seated customer stands up and turns toward me as if trying to get my attention or follow up on her order.

I approach the customer as she waits by the wall, and she signals for attention before following me to the table. The customer then sits down and places a small item on the table, while I remain nearby to assist or take her order.
I approach a waiting customer and guide her to the seating area, where she follows along and sits down at the table to settle in and wait for service.
I approach and guide a customer through the cafe seating area, likely helping her find a place to sit, while she follows to the table and sits down to wait for service.

I approach a customer standing by the wall and guide them toward the seating area, likely helping them get settled at a table. The customer follows along, reaches the chair, and sits down, intending to take a seat and wait to be served.
I approach a waiting customer and guide her toward the seating area, indicating a table for her. The customer follows, pulls out a chair, and sits down to be served or wait there.

I guide the customer from the wall area to the table and indicate a chair so she can be seated. The customer follows my direction, approaches the table, and sits down, appearing ready to wait or be served.
I walk over to the seated customer at the table to check on or take her order, while she waits calmly and looks toward me, appearing ready to speak or place an order.

I walk over to check on a seated customer at a table, likely to take or confirm her order, while she stays seated and looks toward me, appearing to wait for service and briefly gesture to get attention.
I walk over to the seated customer to check on or take her order, while she sits waiting at the table and looks toward me expectantly.
I approach the seated customer’s table to check on or take their order, while the customer looks toward me and raises a hand to get my attention and request service.
I walk over to a seated customer at a table to check on or take her order, while she waits and then raises her hand to get my attention and request service.
I walk over to the seated customer’s table to check on or take her order, while she sits waiting and then raises her hand to get my attention and signal that she wants service.

I walk over to a seated customer at a table and gesture toward the tabletop as if checking on or assisting her order. The customer stays seated, looks at me, and raises a hand to get attention or respond to the service.
I walk over to a seated customer to check on or take her order, while she repeatedly raises and extends her arm to get my attention and indicate she wants assistance.
I walk over to the seated customer’s table to check on or speak with her, while she remains seated waiting and briefly touches her forehead as if thinking or waiting for service.
I walk over to the seated customer’s table to assist or take her order, while she remains seated looking toward me and waiting for service.
I walk over to a seated customer at a table, apparently to check on or take her order. The customer stays seated, looks toward me, and appears to be waiting for service.
I walk over to the seated customer at the table to check on or speak with her, while she stays seated, looks toward me, and appears to be waiting for assistance or responding to my approach.
I walk toward a seated customer at a table to check on or take her order, while she raises her hand to get my attention and request service.
